text.title: Niche Evaluation (Market Researcher) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Niche Evaluation (Market Researcher) application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Niche definition uses the formula '[audience] who do [job] when [trigger]'. Vague definitions ('developers' / 'creators') are rejected.
rule.rationale: Vague niches cannot be sized or addressed; the formula forces a falsifiable handle.

rule.statement: TAM/SAM/SOM rows MUST cite ≥1 named source per row. Uncited round-number figures are rejected.
rule.rationale: Uncited sizings are training-data echoes; cited sizings survive stakeholder review.

rule.statement: Competitor matrix lists ≥3 named competitors with price, segment, and quality estimate. 'No competitors' is treated as a red flag and triggers re-search.
rule.rationale: Zero competitors usually means undefined niche or zero demand, not blue ocean.

rule.statement: Audience-accessibility scoring names ≥2 channels (subreddit / newsletter / conf / Slack) with estimated CAC band. Placeholder values are rejected.
rule.rationale: Unaccessible niches are unsellable regardless of size.

rule.statement: Report ends with a single verdict (GO / PASS / PIVOT) and a one-sentence rationale citing the weakest scoring dimension.
rule.rationale: Reports without a verdict get re-litigated; the verdict pins the decision.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
